title: PHP var_dump
content:
### PHP var_dump

`var_dump()` displays structured information about one or more expressions including type and value.

### Basic Usage

```php
<?php
$str = "Hello";
$int = 42;
$bool = true;
$arr = [1, 2, 3];
$null = null;

var_dump($str);  // string(5) "Hello"
var_dump($int);  // int(42)
var_dump($bool); // bool(true)
var_dump($arr);  // array(3) { [0]=> int(1) ... }
var_dump($null); // NULL
?>
```

### Related Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `var_dump()` | Detailed type and value output |
| `var_export()` | Returns parsable PHP code |
| `print_r()` | Human-readable array/object info |
| `debug_zval_refs()` | Reference count information |

> **Key Point:** `var_dump()` is the best tool for debugging because it shows both type and value, unlike `print_r()` which only shows value.
codeExample:
"Alice", "scores" => [95, 87, 92], "active" => true ]; echo "
";
var_dump($data);
echo "
"; echo "
";
var_export($data);
echo "
"; ?>

quiz.question: Which function outputs parsable PHP code?
